text: Mitigating toxic NO emissions remains a primary challenge in environmental catalysis, particularly for vehicle aftertreatment systems. In this study, we utilize an atom-trapping approach to synthesize a novel class of ceria-supported catalysts featuring atomically dispersed Ru(II) ions. Characterization by HAADF-STEM, EXAFS, and DFT calculations confirms the presence of isolated $Ru_1O_5$ sites localized on the (100) facets of ceria, an environment distinct from the square-planar coordination typically observed for Pt and Pd single atoms. These $Ru_1/CeO_2$ materials exhibit exceptional activity for NO oxidation, significantly outperforming their Pt and Pd counterparts and maintaining stability in the presence of moisture and under hydrothermal aging up to 750 °C. Furthermore, the catalysts demonstrate unprecedented NOx storage capacity, achieving full NO consumption at 100 °C with low Ru loadings (0.05–0.5 wt%). Mechanistic investigations using in situ DRIFTS, mass spectrometry, and modulation-excitation spectroscopy reveal that Ru(II) ions act as unique "shuttles" that facilitate efficient NOx spill-over onto the ceria support as nitrites and nitrates, while also forming stable Ru-nitrosyl complexes. Additionally, $Ru_1/CeO_2$ shows high efficiency in the reduction of NO by CO at low temperatures. We identified the direct participation of oxygen vacancies at the Ru-Ce interface as a critical factor in the reaction mechanism. These findings highlight the potential of dilute single-atom Ru catalysts as cost-effective, high-performance alternatives for the next generation of emission control technologies.
